name: Pop-Up Ad (Historical)
category: Traffic / Advanced — Historical Reference (NOT a build target)
buildableNote: DO NOT BUILD. The original 1990s pop-up email-grab is documented only to explain why the modern squeeze page replaced it. Pop-up blockers killed its viability. If a request matches this intent, redirect to the Squeeze Page Funnel.

goals: Reference/teaching only: explain the historical ancestor of the squeeze page and the modern lead funnel.
signals: User is asking about funnel HISTORY or the origin of squeeze pages.
User literally requests a 'pop-up' lead grab — recognize it, then route to a modern Squeeze Page Funnel.
antiSignals: Any real lead-capture build request — never instantiate this; use Squeeze Page / Lead Magnet funnel instead.

pageStructure.purpose: A browser pop-up window that appeared on a page asking for an email in exchange for a promise. Obsolete due to pop-up blockers.
elements: Submit button delivering the promised item
[Obsolete: blocked by modern browsers — replaced by the inline squeeze page]

copyFramework.primaryPersona: Jim Edwards — Copywriting Secrets persona (The Copy Mechanic): the Big Promise headline formulas, fascination/curiosity bullets, the order-bump and OTO close formulas, the 'one-sentence' hook.
supportingPersonas: Russell Brunson — DotCom Secrets persona (The Funnel Architect): Star/Story/Solution sales letter script, the Value Ladder, Who/What/Why/How lead logic, Soap Opera Sequence + Daily Seinfeld follow-up.
scripts: Historical: a single curiosity Big-Promise line traded for an email (the seed of every squeeze-page headline today).
Modern replacement: build a curiosity-headline Squeeze Page Funnel (DotCom Secrets) instead.
copyFramework.notes: Documented for lineage only. The persuasion DNA (curiosity promise -> email) survives in the squeeze page; the DELIVERY mechanism (intrusive pop-up window) is dead.

pages: NONE. Do not provision. If matched, instantiate a Squeeze Page Funnel in GHL instead (Step 1 opt-in -> Step 2 thank-you).
skill44Widgets: N/A. (Modern equivalent: a Skill 44 opt-in Form on a real squeeze page, or a GHL on-page exit-intent/pop-up FORM element — which is a widget, not a standalone funnel.)
automations: N/A for historical; modern squeeze -> Soap Opera Sequence.
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 must NOT build this. Treat it as a router: detect the historical/pop-up intent, explain the lineage, and redirect to the Squeeze Page Funnel template.
